assessment: Temporal span diffusion produced a small positive signal: strict mouse-button exact TP 1 and F1 0.005263157894736842 on 5k target rows.
No-button FPR remains under the 0.10 gate (0.047971999176446366), but predicted button events increased to 237 with many false positives.
Keyboard key accuracy remains zero and the 2k semantic diagnostic prefix still has zero semantic overlap, so this is only a weak direction signal, not a useful IDM.
next_recipe_faithful_actions: Keep temporal span diffusion, but add span-aware calibration that optimizes held-out exact TP/F-beta under the no-button FPR <=0.10 constraint rather than using raw current-offset span probabilities.
Split key press/release into an analogous temporal span head because keyboard accuracy remains zero.
Run a smaller calibration-only recovery from this checkpoint before another training launch if checkpoint/predictions remain on the PVC.
